_provenance.method: Langmuir and Freundlich isotherm fitting for P adsorption on biochar
_provenance.reproduction_note: Re-run baseline_command to regenerate expected values
references: Kumari, Dong & Safferman (2025) Phosphorus adsorption and recovery from waste streams using biochar. Applied Water Science 15(7):162
Langmuir (1918) The adsorption of gases on plane surfaces. J Am Chem Soc 40:1361-1403
Freundlich (1906) Über die Adsorption in Lösungen. Z Phys Chem 57:385-470
Yao et al. (2011) Biochar from anaerobically digested sugar beet tailings: characterization and phosphate removal. J Environ Qual 40:767-774
Takaya et al. (2016) Phosphate and ammonium sorption capacity of biochar. Chemosphere 145:518-527
_provenance._tolerance_justification: Ranges accommodate nonlinear fitting variability — linearized LS vs scipy.curve_fit may converge to slightly different optima. R² > 0.95 threshold from Kumari et al. (2025) Table 3.

isotherm_data.description: Representative phosphorus adsorption data from biochar studies

wood_biochar_500C.source: Representative of Takaya et al. (2016) and Kumari et al. (2025) wood-based biochar at 500°C

sugar_beet_biochar.source: Representative of Yao et al. (2011) sugar beet tailings biochar

langmuir_fit.description: Langmuir model: qe = qmax * KL * Ce / (1 + KL * Ce)

model_comparison.description: Compare Langmuir vs Freundlich fit quality

checks.description: Langmuir R² >= Freundlich R² for plateau data (typical)
Residuals show no systematic pattern (mean residual < 0.5 mg/g)

separation_factor.description: Langmuir separation factor RL = 1/(1+KL*C0) indicates favorability
